name: Pico Pienzu Trail from Mirador del Fitu
name_original: Pico Pienzu y Bustaca — ida y vuelta a Mirador del Fitu
description: Hike the Pico Pienzu y Bustaca loop from Mirador del Fitu for panoramic views of the Cantabrian Sea and Picos de Europa.
region_intro: Hiking around Caravia Asturias offers diverse landscapes, from the rugged peaks of the Picos de Europa to the scenic Cantabrian Sea coastline. The region features a variety of terrain, including coastal paths, mountain ascents, and forested areas. This unique geographical position provides panoramic views encompassing both the mountains and the sea. Best hiking trails in Caravia The most popular hiking route is Pico Pienzu Trail from Mirador del Fitu, a 7.2 miles (11.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 5 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers panoramic views of both the Cantabrian Sea and the Picos de Europa. Another top favourite among local hikers is Northern Way (Asturias), a moderate 10.1 miles (16.3 km) path. This section of the historic pilgrimage route showcases coastal views and diverse landscapes. Local hikers also love the View of the Cantabrian Sea – La Beciella Beach loop from Santiago de Gobiendes, a 4.2 miles (6.8 km) trail leading through coastal areas with views of La Beciella Beach, often completed in about 1 hour 50 minutes. Hiking around Caravia is defined by coastal paths, mountain ascents, and diverse terrain, offering routes with varying elevation gain and difficulty. The network provides options for different ability levels, from easy coastal loops to more challenging mountain trails. The routes in Caravia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 190 reviews. More than 1500 hikers have used komoot to explore Caravia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Caravia?
A: There are over 20 hiking trails in the Caravia region, offering a diverse range of experiences. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 190 reviews.

Q: What kind of landscapes and views can I expect while hiking in Caravia?
A: Caravia offers a unique blend of landscapes, allowing you to experience both the rugged peaks of the Picos de Europa and the expansive blue of the Cantabrian Sea. Many trails provide panoramic vistas encompassing both mountains and coast. You'll find coastal paths, mountain ascents, and varied terrain throughout the region.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or a relaxed walk?
A: Yes, Caravia features 8 easy hiking trails. For a relaxed coastal walk, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40321603" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Cantabrian Sea – La Beciella Beach loop from Caravia&lt;/a&gt;, which is 5.8 km long and offers pleasant views of the coastline.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of both the Picos de Europa and the Cantabrian Sea?
A: Absolutely. The region is renowned for trails that provide this stunning dual perspective. A prime example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1314922431/pico-pienzu-trail-from-mirador-del-fitu" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Pienzu Trail from Mirador del Fitu&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic views of both the Cantabrian Sea and the Picos de Europa.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to look out for on the trails?
A: While hiking in Caravia, you can encounter several interesting natural features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/795787" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Fito&lt;/a&gt; is a popular viewpoint offering extensive vistas. You might also find unique geological sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5571254" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dinosaur footprints in La Griega beach&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4203160" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Marina Beach&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any sections of the Camino del Norte pilgrimage route in Caravia?
A: Yes, a section of the historic Camino del Norte (Northern Way) passes through the Caravia area. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047502658/northern-way-asturias" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Northern Way (Asturias)&lt;/a&gt; route, a moderate 16.3 km path, showcases coastal views, beaches, and unique murals, offering a blend of natural beauty and cultural interest.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Caravia?
A: Caravia offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. The spring and autumn months generally provide mild temperatures and beautiful scenery, ideal for exploring both coastal and mountain trails. Summer is also popular, especially for coastal walks, though it can be warmer.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Caravia?
A: Yes, Caravia offers several circular routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6972263" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Cantabrian Sea – La Beciella Beach loop from Santiago de Gobiendes&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 6.8 km trail leading through coastal areas with views of La Beciella Beach.

Q: What are the options for parking near trailheads in Caravia?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. For popular starting points like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/795787" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Fito&lt;/a&gt;, there are designated parking areas. For other routes, roadside parking or small village car parks may be available. It's advisable to check specific route details for parking information.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Caravia?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars. Reviewers often praise the region's unique combination of stunning coastal and mountain views, the variety of terrain, and the well-maintained paths that cater to different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Caravia?
A: Many of Caravia's easier coastal loops are suitable for families. These trails often have less elevation gain and offer engaging features like beach access or scenic viewpoints. Always check the specific route details for distance and terrain to ensure it's appropriate for your family's abilities.

Q: Can I reach hiking trails in Caravia using public transport?
A: Public transport options to specific trailheads in Caravia can be limited. While some villages and towns in the region are served by buses, direct access to more remote starting points might require a car. It's recommended to research local bus schedules if you plan to rely on public transport for your hike.
tips: The start of the walk begins at the parking lot. One of the first stops may be the Fitu viewpoint. Panoramic view of the peaks that you can follow along this trail, towards the high elevations of Pico Pienzu.

Hiking area through the Sierra de Fitu. The Whip Hills are home to local herds of wild horses and cows. The views of the peaks are incredible on a clear day. This hike is at elevation and the weather could be very different from the valleys below, so be prepared.

In the Sueve there are two reference peaks: Pienzu, the highest, most famous and most traveled, and Miruenu, an almost twin peak, much less accessible, and logically almost deserted, but in my opinion much more beautiful and with the same or better views than from the Pienzu.
EYE to the area marked as full of ticks.

faqs.question: Where does the Pico Pienzu y Bustaca trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The trail officially begins at the Mirador del Fitu, which is easily accessible by car. There is parking available directly at the viewpoint, serving as a convenient trailhead for your hike.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this hike?
faqs.answer: Hikers generally take between 3 and 5 hours to complete the round trip. This can vary based on your fitness level and how much time you spend enjoying the panoramic views along the way.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the route to Pico Pienzu?
faqs.answer: The initial section of the trail involves a relatively flat stretch through mountain pastures. As you ascend, the terrain becomes more rocky, with limestone formations. The final climb to Pico Pienzu is the most demanding, becoming quite steep with the path sometimes disappearing among rocks. Sturdy hiking shoes and trekking poles are highly recommended.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect from this trail?
faqs.answer: This route is renowned for its spectacular panoramic views. From the Mirador del Fitu, you'll see the Cantabrian coast and mountain ranges like the Picos de Europa. As you climb, especially from Pienzu Peak, you can enjoy expansive 360-degree vistas, including over 200 kilometers of coastline on clear days.

faqs.question: Is the Pico Pienzu y Bustaca trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on this trail. However, as the route passes through the Paisaje Protegido de la Sierra del Sueve, it is crucial to keep your dog on a leash at all times to protect local wildlife and livestock, especially the Asturcón horses.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike is typically during spring, summer, and early autumn when the weather is more stable and the chances of clear skies are higher, offering the best visibility for the panoramic views. Always check the weather forecast before heading out, as conditions can change rapidly in the mountains.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Pico Pienzu y Bustaca trail. However, as it is located within the Paisaje Protegido de la Sierra del Sueve, please respect all environmental regulations, such as staying on marked paths and not disturbing wildlife.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or points of interest will I encounter?
faqs.answer: Beyond the stunning views from the Mirador del Fitu and Pienzu Peak, you'll pass through the ancient pastoral settlement of Mayada de Bustacu, where you can see old stone cabins. There's also the Mergullines fountain, a good spot to refill water.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I see along the trail?
faqs.answer: The Sierra del Sueve is rich in biodiversity. You might spot emblematic local wildlife such as Asturcón horses (a wild breed), fallow deer, vultures, and choughs. Keep an eye out, especially in the quieter sections of the trail.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other paths. You will encounter parts of the [CIMA AS24] El Fito * Les Arriondes, [CIMA AS24] El Fito * Loroñe, and Camino de Cofiño a El Bustaco trails along the way.

faqs.question: Is there water available on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, there is a natural water source at the Mergullines fountain (at 870m), which can be a good spot to refill your water bottle. However, it's always advisable to carry sufficient water for your entire hike, especially during warmer months.
